title: DrawdownProtectionRule treats realized PnL as account balance

evidence.quote: peakBalance is hard-coded to 10000 while currentBalance starts at zero and accumulates only closed-trade PnL.
For an open position, currentBalance is replaced by CostBasis rather than equity including cash and unrealized PnL.
The covered positive case only asserts that a losing trade triggers the rule.

reasoning: Because currentBalance is PnL rather than initial capital plus PnL, a break-even closed trade leaves currentBalance at zero and produces a 100% drawdown from the hard-coded 10000 peak. The rule can halt trading when there has been no drawdown, and it cannot track true peak equity over a sequence.
reproduction: Record a BUY at 100 and SELL at 100, then evaluate NewDrawdownProtectionRule(0.1); the rule reports a 100% drawdown even though realized PnL is zero.
recommendation: Track equity as initial capital plus realized and unrealized PnL, update peak over the equity path, and make initial capital configurable or shared with the record/account model.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests cover no-trade false and one losing-trade true only. They do not cover break-even trades, profitable trades, or drawdown below threshold.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add cases for break-even and profitable closed trades that must not trigger, plus a controlled equity path with a drawdown just below and just above the threshold.
